splashes: Badgers are part of the Mustelidae family.
Badgers have stocky bodies with short legs that are suitable for digging.
Badgers on average weigh around 9 - 11 kg.
The badger can run up to 30 km/h.
A male badger is called a boar.
A female badger is called a sow.
A baby badger is called a cub.
A group of badgers is called a cete.
Badgers were eaten in Britain during World War II
Badgers can grow up to 34 inches long.
Badgers mate any time they feel like it.
The badger is the state animal of Wisconsin.
The American badger spends most of its time alone.
The earliest recorded use of the word badger was in 1523.
Badgers have a very powerful sense of smell.
